commit.message:
feat: chunk large documents at sync time for better RAG accuracy

Documents > 60k tokens (the per-doc context cap) were previously
invisible to Claude. Now they are split into overlapping ~10k-token
chunks stored as separate Firestore documents.

lib/chunker.ts (new)
- CHUNK_THRESHOLD_TOKENS = 60k (only docs blocked by the per-doc cap)
- CHUNK_SIZE_CHARS = 40k chars (~10k tokens), overlap = 2k chars
- Snaps chunk boundaries to paragraph breaks where possible
- saveChunks() / deleteChunks() batched Firestore helpers

lib/document-selector.ts
- DocMeta/SelectedDocument get isChunk, isChunked, parentDocumentId,
  chunkIndex, totalChunks fields
- allDocumentNames catalog filters out chunk docs (shows parents only)
- Selection loop: skips isChunked parents, limits MAX_CHUNKS_PER_PARENT=3
  per parent to prevent a single manual flooding the context window

app/api/admin/drive/route.ts
- After parse: if needsChunking(), store parent with 1k-char excerpt,
  then saveChunks() with full content
- On re-sync: deleteChunks(old chunkIds) before writing new ones

app/api/admin/upload/route.ts
- Same chunking logic for manually uploaded files

lib/claude.ts
- formatDocumentsForContext adds chunk="N/total" XML attribute so
  Claude knows it is reading a partial section of a larger document

A 225k-token PDF (ПУЕ.pdf) becomes 22 × ~10k-token chunks.
Re-sync those files from Admin → Drive to activate chunking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add IPU6 camera (ov01a10) support for Ubuntu 25.10

commit.message:
feat(state): continuous state externalization to survive context compaction (v3.10.4)

New lib/state.sh writes ORCHESTRATOR_STATE.md at key lifecycle moments
(agent launch, status poll, merge) so reinject-context.sh has a fresh
snapshot when Claude Code compacts the session. Reduces the gap between
last known state and compaction to ~30s instead of losing all in-flight
reasoning.

commit.message:
feat: centralized config, agent provisioning, release pipeline, renam… (#223)

* feat: centralized config, agent provisioning, release pipeline, rename webui to controlplane

- Remote configuration mode: agents fetch settings from control plane with 3-tier merge
- Agent provisioning UI: create agent + API key in one step, shows download instructions
- Release pipeline: GitHub Actions builds cross-platform agentctl binaries on tag push
- Version injection via ldflags (agentctl --version)
- /releases/latest proxy endpoint with 5-min cache for download links
- DownloadInstructions component with platform detection and copy-paste setup
- Rename docs/webui/ to docs/controlplane/, remove all "webui" terminology
- API key-bound agent identity: agent name/type derived from key
- Config pages: global type config + per-agent overrides with merge preview
- DB migrations for config tables and API key agent identity columns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add cmd/controlplane/main.go previously blocked by gitignore

The bare "controlplane" gitignore entry was matching the cmd/controlplane/
directory. This was fixed to "/controlplane" in the previous commit but
the file was not staged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gitignore bare "agentctl" was excluding cmd/agentctl/ directory

Changed to "/agentctl" so it only matches the root binary, not the
cmd/agentctl/ source directory. Same fix as the previous controlplane
gitignore issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: extract syscall.Statfs into platform-specific files for Windows build

syscall.Statfs_t and syscall.Statfs are Unix-only. Split disk space
checks into build-tagged files (_unix.go, _windows.go) using
GetDiskFreeSpaceExW on Windows.

feat: thread hostname through connect_existing and config

connect_existing() was hardcoding git@github.com URLs. Now accepts a
`host` parameter (default: "github.com") and builds URLs using it.

Changes:
- config.py: add github.host default ("github.com") and PURSER_GITHUB_HOST
  env var override
- connect_existing(): new `host` param, URL uses git@{host}:{owner}/{repo}

Initial release: Python Loops interactive learning guide

10-page progressive web learning experience teaching while/for loops
to SCQF Level 6 students. Pure HTML/CSS/vanilla JS, no dependencies.

- Pages 1-3: Foundations (why loops, while anatomy, infinite loops)

nav bar, drag-drop reorder, robot maze game engine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: make URL parsing host-agnostic for GitHub Enterprise support

_parse_github_url() was hardcoded to match only github.com hostnames,
which breaks detection on GitHub Enterprise instances (e.g.
github.wellsfargo.com, github.corp.example.com).

Changes:
- Regexes now match any hostname in git@<host>:, https://<host>/, and
  ssh://git@<host>/ URL patterns
- Return type changed from (owner, repo) to (host, owner, repo)
- Remote dicts now include a "host" field throughout the detection chain
  (detect_github_remotes, _detect_via_gh_cli, create_repo, connect_existing)
- All callers updated for the new 3-tuple return shape

Closes: purser-m1j

JP copy style guide compliance + audit form enhancements + middleware fix

Japanese copy (ja.ts, audit-ja.ts):
- Remove 15 keigo violations (お選びいただけます→選べます, お渡しします→あなたのものです, etc.)
- Replace ソリューション x3 with native Japanese (解決策, 提案)
- Replace unnecessary katakana: パラダイムシフト→発想の転換, ベストバリュー→いちばんお得,
  コミットメント→プラン, エンゲージメント→契約/プロジェクト, ナレッジサイロ→知識の属人化
- Add onomatopoeia for native feel: サクッと, スッと, グッと
- Apply hiragana softening: つくります, かんたん, ずっと
- Fix footer tagline from English imperative structure to natural Japanese
- Remove お客様/御社 from marketing copy (kept in privacy policy)
- Reduce exclamation point overuse in audit sidebar

Audit form (CTO agent):
- Add data maturity step, expanded operations, AI experience questions
- Remove deprecated workflow-demo component
- Middleware simplified to NextResponse.next() passthrough

Users can pick a favorite track when rating a multi-track release. The

load, and displayed on profile pages. The <select> dropdown is suppressed
for single-track releases.

feat: add brand management page with edit, delete, and re-scrape

Brand page (/brand) inside (dashboard) route group:
- Lists all user's brands as cards with key audit stats
  (visibility score, audit count, mention rate, recommendation progress)
- Edit modal: update brand name, URL, overview, differentiators (chips),
  and competitors (chips) with Enter-to-add UX
- Delete with confirmation dialog — cascades through all related data
  (recommendations → audit_results → audits → prompts → workspace)
- Re-scrape: re-fetches website content via GPT-4o to refresh brand
  profile data (overview, differentiators, competitors)
- Clicking brand name navigates to dashboard with that workspace active
- Empty state with CTA for first-time users
- Animated modals with scale+translate open/close transitions

API endpoints created:
- PATCH /api/workspaces/[id] — update workspace fields (allowlisted)
- DELETE /api/workspaces/[id] — cascade-delete workspace and all children
- POST /api/workspaces/[id]/rescrape — re-scrape website and update profile

All endpoints verify user ownership before performing operations.
